description: §51.0.J / §51.0.E E-DERIVED-ENGINE-NO-INITIAL — `initial=` declared on a derived engine. `<engine for=Health initial=.Healthy derived=match @marioState {...}>` carries both the derivation source AND a static initial variant; the two are mutually exclusive because a derived engine's initial value is computed from `derived=expr` at engine-init time. Asserts the code AS error. W-ENGINE-INITIAL-MISSING is in notCodes — that lint is the NON-derived counterpart (§51.0.E) and must not co-fire.
rationale: §51.0.J rules table: '`initial=` on the engine | REJECTED — `E-DERIVED-ENGINE-NO-INITIAL` (§34). Initial value computed from `derived=expr` at engine-init time.' §51.0.E: 'On a DERIVED engine (§51.0.J), `initial=` is FORBIDDEN. Derived engines compute their initial value from the source expression at engine-init time. Setting `initial=` emits `E-DERIVED-ENGINE-NO-INITIAL` (§34).' §34 catalog: 'Initial value computed from `derived=expr` at engine-init time; remove `initial=`.'
